title: Peppery Chewy German Pappardelle Pasta
description: A hearty German-inspired dish featuring chewy pappardelle pasta, infused with a spicy peppery flavor. Perfect for a quick and energizing lunch or dinner.
instructions: Heat the grapeseed oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the onion and garlic and cook until the onion is translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the freshly ground black pepper, coarse black pepper, and cayenne pepper, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chicken broth and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Add the pappardelle pasta and cook, stirring frequently, until the pasta is al dente, about 8-10 minutes.
Add the whey, unsalted butter, and salt to the pasta and stir until the butter has melted and the pasta is coated in the sauce.
Stir in the ground mustard and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until the sauce has thickened slightly.
Stir in the fresh parsley and parmesan cheese, and cook until the cheese has melted and the pasta is coated in the sauce.
Serve the pappardelle pasta hot, garnished with additional parsley and parmesan cheese if desired.
Let the dish rest for 2-3 minutes before serving.
Slice the dish into 4-6 portions, depending on serving size.
Serve the dish hot, garnished with additional parsley and parmesan cheese if desired.
